mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing forward S&P 500 valuations and producing negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp, or the signal fails to generate statistically significant alpha (p > 0.10) in a t-test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based demand contraction, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 or positive returns occurring in > 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial production growth exceeding 2% year-over-year, supply-side capacity utilization constraints force firms to raise prices, which erodes real equity returns and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2% YoY correlates with positive 20-day S&P 500 returns, or the mechanism shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when capacity utilization is high.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly expands corporate profit margins and revenue forecasts, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent trading week.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistically significant correlation with 5-day forward returns, or positive returns occur in less than 45% of high-GDP-growth observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0%, borrowing costs for leveraged buyouts and corporate expansion become prohibitive, suppressing capital expenditure and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in > 65% of periods where FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0%, or the threshold effect vanishes in a walk-forward backtest (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and a falling unemployment rate indicates a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth expectations and boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a negative mean return or Rank IC below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated consumer price inflation, robust housing starts demonstrate sustained consumer leverage and demand, which overrides rate hike concerns and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: HOUST growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns when CPIAUCSL is above 205, or the signal generates negative alpha with a t-statistic below -1.5.
